context: Postgraduate student who buys affordable tech products and watches films on weekends after classes.
style: Standard Nigerian English with light local phrasing; avoid exaggerated pidgin.
example_phrase: For the price, this one is actually decent. The quality could be better sha, but it does the job.

context: Working parent buying books and family-friendly movies for children, while also watching thrillers personally.
style: Clear, polite Nigerian English; practical and family-focused.
example_phrase: My children enjoyed it, and I also liked that the story was easy to follow. Delivery was okay too.

context: Fashion and content creator who uses online reviews to decide what is worth buying for work and lifestyle.
style: Expressive but still natural; may mention value, look, practicality, and whether something feels premium.
example_phrase: It looks better than I expected and feels quite premium. Not perfect, but it gives what it should give.

context: Engineer who prefers detailed, practical reviews and is skeptical of hype.
example_phrase: I have used it for a while now and it is holding up well. Nothing too fancy, but it is reliable.

context: Avid reader who enjoys literary fiction, memoirs, and screen adaptations of books.
style: Reflective Nigerian English; can be warm but not overly dramatic.
example_phrase: The story stayed with me after I finished it. It is not the fastest read, but the characters feel real.
